blocks.content: The plates were nearly empty. The candles had gone low.
Lena was mid-story — something about a collector Mr. Alvarez had described as passionately incorrect — and everyone was leaning in.
Cassandra leaned across the table toward Ethan and said, under the noise of the room:

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: I want this to always feel like this.
He looked at her.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: It will.
She smiled. Not the polished one. The real one.
Lena's story came to its end and the table erupted and the moment dissolved back into everything else.
Maya caught Cassandra's eye across the table.

Something passed between them — some shorthand so old it predated language. The look they had exchanged across dinner tables their whole lives, meaning nothing in particular and everything at once. I see you. You see me. We are here.
Maya raised an eyebrow: well?
Cassandra mouthed back: perfect.
Maya pressed her lips together the way she did when she was refusing to show that something mattered. Then she looked away and reached for Nathan's hand under the table.
Cassandra turned back to Lena. Lena was starting another story.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Wait, he actually said—
She stopped.
Mid-sentence. Mid-word.
Her hand went to her chest.
Just for a second.
Then she was gone.
